title: viola betonicifolia subsp. nova-guineensis
intro: Viola betonicifolia subsp. nova-guineensis, commonly known as the New Guinea pansy, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the violet family that is native to New Guinea. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The New Guinea pansy has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. It can be prepared in various forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health conditions.
